title: FAQ TechOffice
content:
Frequently Asked Questions 
Tech Office: techno@webb.org  
 
 
What does the school  do about anti -virus software? 
The school requires anti-virus software be installed on all student computers brought on campus. The tech 
office can assist students, but it is the student’s responsibility to maintain their systems and keep them 
updated with the latest anti-virus and security updates. 
 
Does the school keep my student’s machine from  getting viruses, malware,  or 
spyware?  
The school does its best to keep the network virus free. Unfortunately, the latest viruses, malware and 
spyware require every person with a digital device to be responsible for keeping the network free of 
viruses. Each machine needs to be updated with all critical security updates, checked regularly for 
spyware, and have anti-virus software scanning the system constantly. 
 
What happens  if a computer is infected  with a virus, malware  or spyware? 
As soon as a machine is detected with a worm, virus, or spyware it is disconnected from the network. 
When a student realizes that their machine has been disconnected from the network, they should bring 
the machine to the Fawcett Library tech office so that the tech team can verify the machine has been 
cleaned properly. Once the machine has been cleared by the tech office, the machine will be allowed back 
on the network. 
Can students  use or setup wireless  access  on campus? 
Students are not allowed to use wireless routers or access points in the dorms. When not configured 
properly, wireless devices can cause issues for the rest of the users in the dorms. 
 
What types of computers  do students  use at The Webb Schools? 
Students can use a Mac or PC for their school work. Most tools used by Webb are browser based and do 
not require any special setup or hardware. 
Has Webb standardized  on any particular  software?  
Office 365 by Microsoft is used throughout the school for word processing, spreadsheet, and presentation 
tasks. Webb provides an MS Office 365 license to each student that requests one, which allows the student 
to install the software on up to 5 devices. Many classes require that assignments be turned in 
electronically. These files must be in the Microsoft Office format. If students choose to use other software 
than MS Office 365 they are responsible for saving the files in the standard MS Office formats of docx, xlsx, 
and pptx. 
Are students  required  to have computers? 
Yes. Students are asked to have a personal laptop available to them for when teachers ask them to bring 
one to class and to work with while on campus. Webb does provide public access computers with 
specialized software.

Can I contact  my child  via email or instant  messaging?  
Yes. Every student has access to a personal email account. Check with your student for his or her email 
address. Typically, it is firstinitiallastname@webb.org, for example Jane Smith could be reached at 
jsmith@webb.org. 
 
How do I access Canvas? 
https://webb.instructure.com 
How do I access SIS? 
https://webb.myschoolapp.com 
How do I remotely access my Office 365 applications (Word, Excel, Outlook, OneDrive)? 
 
https://www.office.com 
How do I access Adobe? 
https://www.adobe.com 
How can I securely manage the passwords to all of my portals? 
 
LastPass: https://lastpass.com 
You can sign up for the free version of LastPass. Once the sign up is complete, you will be required to install 
a browser plugin. You will then be able to access your portals using a single, encrypted password. LastPass 
works with all major browsers. 
 
I received a new phone and now my number changed. How do I change that in my 
Microsoft portal?  
 
How-to Setup Microsoft Authentication 
 
Instead of using your phone number, we recommend the Microsoft Authenticator app. Download the app 
from the Google Play or Apple store. Next, log into your O365 portal. At the top-right corner, click your 
initials, then My Account from the drop-down menu. In the new window click Office apps. The screen will 
change again with a new menu. Select Security & privacy from the menu, Additional security verification, 
then Update your phone numbers used for account security. At the top of this page will be a new drop-down 
menu. Depending on your version, choose Mobile App or Use verification code from app or token. Select the 
button to Set up Authenticator app and follow the prompts to scan the QR code. 
 
 
My portal page is blank when logging in, why is this? 
 
On occasion you may notice that the portal you log into is blank immediately after entering your password. 
This is common when using Google Chrome on a Mac. To remedy the issue, you will need to set your 
Chrome settings back to defaults. To do this, click the 3 dots in the top-right corner of the browser and 
select Settings from the menu. On the left side click Advanced, then System. Look for the option to Restore 
settings to their original defaults. Click the prompt to reset if necessary. Once this is complete, close 
Chrome, wait a few seconds, then open Chrome again. You should now be able to log into your portals 
correctly.

Where should I be saving my files?  
 
Files are best stored in your Microsoft Office 365 OneDrive. If you lose your device or the hard drive fails, 
you can still retrieve your files from anywhere if you have an Internet connection. Log into your Office 365 
portal and navigate to the OneDrive link. Data is uploaded as easy as dragging and dropping into the page. 
 
I should be part of a school group, but it’s not showing in my email, what do I do? 
 
Please contact tech at techno@webb.org. We can add you to the group. Note that in some cases it may take 
up to 24hrs to see a group in Outlook. 
 
Who can I contact  with questions  about Technology?  
Contact the technology department if you have any questions. Please be aware that we cannot 
recommend a specific manufacturer of hardware or software beyond what is described in this document. 
 
The Webb Schools 
1175 West Baseline Road 
Claremont, CA 91711-2199
